title: BEM-877 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM877_WORKER_PROTOCOL_V15_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-877 WORKER PROTOCOL V1.5 REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Findings:
- Operator remarks are valid. v1.4 is usable but too repetitive and has inconsistent terms.
- v1.5 should use object, element, contour, workspace, channel, event log, rulebook as the canonical terms.
- Curator is an element, not only part of Worker. Analyst, Auditor, Executor are also elements.
- Testing must be described as a function assigned to one internal contour and performed in testing workspace. A permanent separate testing contour is not required.
- Common managed channel and global rulebook should be treated as one top-level governance mechanism. Internal channels exist inside each object.
- External exchange between objects goes Curator-to-Curator, not from low-level elements directly.
- Product repositories are not only release locations; the managing contour also upgrades, repairs, extends, and learns from them.
- The protocol should be shorter, with centralized definitions and no duplicate explanations.
Recommendation:
- Approve v1.5 with notes and prepare DOCX for operator review.
No issue comments.
